title: What Exactly is Sha — EML Classification of Ghost Curves

analysis.object: T849: What Exactly is Sha — EML Classification of Ghost Curves depth analysis

sha_definition.description: Sha(E) = kernel of H^1(Q,E) -> prod H^1(Q_v,E) -- local-global failure

sha_element_as_torsor.description: Each sha element is a principal homogeneous space (torsor) for E

ghost_curve.description: Ghost curve: has Q_v-points for all v but no Q-point -- local-global phantom
ghost_curve.reason: EML-inf: no finite collection of local data determines global

sha_depth.description: Sha as a GROUP: torsion abelian group -- EML-1 growth structure

shadow_structure.description: Sha is EML-inf GROUP; |Sha| is EML-0 shadow of that group

t849_theorem.description: T849: Sha(E) is EML-inf (no finite local data determines global). Its cardinality |Sha| is EML-0 (integer shadow). The EML-inf object casts an EML-0 shadow. T849: Sha = EML-inf; |Sha| = EML-0.
t849_theorem.reason: Sha depth: EML-inf. Shadow depth: EML-0.

analysis.theorem: T849: What Exactly is Sha — EML Classification of Ghost Curves (S1129).

key_theorem: T849: What Exactly is Sha — EML Classification of Ghost Curves (S1129).
